posture: Quiet Tuesday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a-§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Recurring items were again reviewed and excluded as out-of-window, out-of-scope or duplicative: the NSW commercial FOGO mandate (source-separated organics for supermarkets/institutions/hospitality from 1 July 2026; household mandate to 2030) is a regulatory-rollout milestone rather than a fresh innovation signal; Hawkesbury City Council's ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial rests on a November 2025 FOGO-deferral decision and a January 2026 trial commencement (prior-period background); Baw Baw Shire's new FOGO green-bin service is a scheduled rollout, not a novel signal; and the NSW AI-in-planning tools ($5.6m Early Adopter Grant Program across 16 councils, DAISY/Civica Altitude integration, AI Solutions Panel, and the 90-day DA determination target from 1 July) produced no new dated development today. Overseas material (Bloomberg CityLab/Mayors Challenge 2025-26 winners, Milton Keynes and Harrow drone-fed digital-twin trials, LGIU/LGC AI briefings) was out-of-window, network/policy content, or already logged as background rather than a fresh, council-transferable signal. Today is a Tuesday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
